title: Beauville's splitting principle for Chow rings of projective hyperkaehler manifolds
speaker: Lie Fu
abstract: Being the natural generalization of K3 surfaces, hyperkaehler varieties, also known as irreducible holomorphic symplectic varieties, are one of the building blocks of smooth projective varieties with trivial canonical bundle. One of the guiding conjectures in the study of algebraic cycles of such varieties is Beauville's splitting principle. Concerning the weak form of the splitting principle, I want to report some progress on the closely related Beauville-Voisin conjecture.

proof_stages.label: Beauville-Voisin K3 prototype
proof_stages.summary: The talk starts from the Beauville-Voisin theorem for a projective K3 surface, where products of divisors and c2(TS) are forced into the distinguished degree-one zero-cycle, and then reinterprets this as a multiplicative Bloch-Beilinson-type splitting of the Chow ring.

proof_stages.label: Multiplicative Bloch-Beilinson-type splitting
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines a multiplicative direct-sum decomposition CH^i(X)=⊕_j CH^i(X)_j, states the basic Bloch-Beilinson-type conditions such as vanishing for negative j and injectivity of the cycle-class map on the j=0 part, and explains the associated expected filtration.

proof_stages.label: Abelian-variety eigenspace splitting and symmetric-divisor injectivity
proof_stages.summary: Beauville's eigenspace decomposition for Chow groups of abelian varieties is recalled as a second multiplicative-splitting model, and later its symmetric-line-bundle subalgebra is used together with the Ancona/Moonen/O'Sullivan injectivity theorem.

proof_stages.label: Hyperkähler splitting principles and Beauville-Voisin conjecture
proof_stages.summary: The speaker formulates Beauville's strong splitting principle for projective hyperkähler varieties, derives the weak divisor-generated injectivity conjecture from it, strengthens it by adjoining Chern classes of the tangent bundle, and lists known cases including the generalized Kummer result to be proved.

proof_stages.label: De Cataldo-Migliorini correspondence reduction
proof_stages.summary: The proof for generalized Kummer varieties begins by recalling the De Cataldo-Migliorini correspondences for Hilbert schemes of points on a smooth projective surface, which give Chow-group isomorphisms and injections into Chow groups of products of the surface.

proof_stages.label: Proof for generalized Kummer varieties
proof_stages.summary: The Hilbert-scheme correspondences are base-changed to the fibers defining generalized Kummer varieties, homologically trivial classes in the algebra generated by divisors and tangent Chern classes are pushed to nonconnected abelian varieties, Voisin's lemma places them in the symmetric-line-bundle subalgebra, and the abelian injectivity theorem forces them to vanish in Chow.

proof_stages.label: Motivic strong splitting via the small diagonal
proof_stages.summary: The second part reformulates the strong splitting principle in the language of Chow motives as a multiplicative Chow-Künneth decomposition governed by the small diagonal, and recalls Vial's theorem giving such a decomposition for Hilbert schemes of points on K3 surfaces.

proof_stages.label: Orbifold Chow/motive theorem for Hilbert schemes of K3 surfaces
proof_stages.summary: The speaker presents joint work with Tian proving a Chow-ring and motivic analogue of the orbifold-cohomology description of Hilbert schemes of K3 surfaces, explaining that the product is controlled by the small diagonal, by De Cataldo-Migliorini correspondences, and by Voisin-style universality arguments.

examples.name: Kummer surface as the n=1 generalized Kummer case
examples.note: The speaker notes that for n=1 the generalized Kummer construction recovers the desingularization of A/{±1}, making the generalized Kummer family concrete without being used in the proof.
examples.engagement_reason: The Kummer surface is only identified as the n=1 fiber/desingularization case of the generalized Kummer construction, with no further work on that surface.

passes.reason: It is the minimal nontrivial n=1 member of the generalized Kummer series, cited as a standard example rather than to refute a claim.

examples.name: Fano variety of lines on a cubic fourfold
examples.note: This is cited as another hyperkähler variety and later as a known case of Voisin's Beauville-Voisin conjecture, serving as evidence for the conjectural picture.
examples.engagement_reason: The Fano variety of lines on a cubic fourfold is cited as a known hyperkähler example and known case of Voisin’s theorem, but no construction or calculation is carried out on it.

passes.reason: The Fano variety of lines on a smooth cubic fourfold is a standard hyperkähler example and known positive case, not a structural exception or counterexample.

examples.name: Product of K3 surfaces
examples.note: In response to a question, the speaker says that a product of K3 surfaces is not irreducible holomorphic symplectic but should still fit the expected multiplicative-decomposition philosophy because the small diagonal controls multiplication.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker uses products of K3 surfaces concretely as S^n with symmetric-group action, forms the quotient stack, identifies the Hilbert scheme as a crepant resolution, and traces the additive/orbifold Chow decomposition via fixed loci and partitions.

passes.reason: A product of K3 surfaces is a decomposable but otherwise standard holomorphic-symplectic example proposed as an expected positive case, not as an obstruction.
